guardrails.title: Monotonic Primary Key
guardrails.body: Use monotonically increasing values such as BIGINT UNSIGNED AUTO_INCREMENT for primary keys in OLTP tables. Because InnoDB physically arranges rows in PK order via the clustered index, random-value PKs (e.g., UUID v4) cause frequent page splits during writes, degrading performance. If an externally visible identifier is needed, place it in a secondary unique column.

guardrails.title: UTF8MB4 Charset Required
guardrails.body: Use utf8mb4 as the character set and utf8mb4_0900_ai_ci (MySQL 8.0+) or utf8mb4_unicode_ci as the collation. MySQL's utf8 stores only up to 3-byte characters, causing emoji and some CJK characters to be silently truncated.

guardrails.title: No ENUM Column Type
guardrails.body: Avoid ENUM types; use lookup tables instead. Adding or removing ENUM values requires ALTER TABLE, and referential integrity via foreign key constraints cannot be enforced. Although displayed as strings, values are stored internally as ordinals, causing unexpected behavior in sorting and application-level comparisons.

guardrails.title: DATETIME Over TIMESTAMP
guardrails.body: Prefer DATETIME over TIMESTAMP for date-time columns. TIMESTAMP has an upper limit of 2038-01-19, and implicit UTC conversion on store / session-timezone conversion on read makes it prone to data inconsistencies when timezone settings differ.

guardrails.title: Composite Index Column Order
guardrails.body: In composite indexes, place columns used in equality conditions (=) first, followed by columns used in range conditions (<, >, BETWEEN) or sorting (ORDER BY). Due to MySQL's leftmost-prefix rule, columns after a range condition cannot be used for index-based filtering or sorting.

guardrails.title: EXPLAIN Verify Query Plan
guardrails.body: Run EXPLAIN (or EXPLAIN ANALYZE where possible) on newly added or modified queries and verify the execution plan. Treat type: ALL (full table scan) or Using filesort / Using temporary in the Extra column as red flags, and consider revising indexes or rewriting the query.

guardrails.title: No Function on Indexed Column
guardrails.body: Do not apply functions or casts to indexed columns in WHERE clauses. Expressions such as WHERE YEAR(created_at) = 2026 disable index usage. Rewrite as WHERE created_at >= '2026-01-01' AND created_at < '2027-01-01' instead.

guardrails.title: Online DDL Preferred
guardrails.body: Prefer online DDL (ALGORITHM=INPLACE / INSTANT) for schema changes on production tables. ALGORITHM=COPY rebuilds the entire table and results in long lock times. For large tables where online DDL is not available (e.g., PK changes), consider third-party tools such as gh-ost or pt-online-schema-change.

guardrails.title: Deadlock Retry with Backoff
guardrails.body: After an InnoDB deadlock (error 1213) triggers an automatic transaction rollback, retry with backoff. As a preventive measure, access rows in a consistent order (e.g., ascending PK) within transactions that update multiple rows to reduce deadlock frequency.

guardrails.title: Batch Write Sizing
guardrails.body: Split large INSERT / UPDATE operations into batches. Target 500–5,000 rows per batch to balance reduced network round trips and limited lock hold time. Operating on tens of thousands of rows in a single statement can cause lock contention and replication lag.

guardrails.title: Prefer UNION ALL
guardrails.body: Use UNION ALL instead of UNION when duplicate elimination is not needed. UNION performs an implicit DISTINCT (sort + deduplication) over the entire result set, incurring unnecessary overhead.

guardrails.title: NOT NULL by Default
guardrails.body: Apply NOT NULL constraints to columns by default. NULL introduces three-valued logic and produces counterintuitive behavior in WHERE clauses, aggregate functions, and unique constraints. Allow NULL only when there is a clear business reason for a value to be absent.

guardrails.title: No Multi-Valued Attribute
guardrails.body: Do not store multiple IDs or values as a comma-separated list or JSON array in a single column. Use a junction table in the relational model instead. Comma-separated storage makes it difficult to search, update, or enforce referential integrity on individual values.
